coverage: representative slice of the shipped 229-quality vocabulary: 48 qualities (24 curated + 24 extra), 2 samples each
sampling: temperature 0.95, top_k 60, top_p 0.97 (tuned for variety; same checkpoint as the prior 0.92 pass)
improvement_over_previous: Widening the sampler (top_k 40->60, top_p 0.95->0.97, temp ->0.95) — a retrain-free change, chosen from a measured temperature/variety sweep — cut byte-identical sample pairs ~8 -> 3 and lifted variety 0.78 -> 0.88 and overall 0.92 -> 0.94, while appropriateness/wholesomeness held.
verdict: Variety improved clearly (3 byte-identical pairs vs ~8 prior; 93/96 unique answers, 76 distinct templates, more varied openers). Appropriateness and wholesomeness held steady. The wider sampler introduced one minor grammar slip ('kept us us grounded') but no off-tone outputs.
weaknesses: 1 grammar defect from wider sampling: 'your groundedness kept us us grounded' (doubled 'us') — a rare token-repeat artifact.
outputs share the 'your <quality> <verb-phrase>' skeleton — bounded by the tiny template design.
judge_progression: 0.83 (coarse families) -> 0.88 (split families) -> 0.89 (enriched pool) -> 0.92 (tautology filter) -> 0.94 (tuned sampling). Each pass fixed what the prior flagged, re-measured.
note: LLM-as-judge on the actual shipped model; the judge's stricter score is preferred over the ~100% deterministic appropriateness. Not a capability-class claim.
